title: pulsatilla vulgaris subsp. anglica
intro: Pulsatilla vulgaris subsp. anglica, also known as the English pasque flower, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and may also aid in menstrual problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, tea, or infused oil, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
